examples.title: Using getpid() and getppid()
examples.description: Demonstrates how to obtain and display PID and PPID
examples.explanation: This program uses getpid() to obtain the current process ID and getppid() to obtain the parent process ID. Both return pid_t type values. When run from shell, the parent is typically the shell process.

examples.title: fork() System Call with PID Handling
examples.description: Shows fork() return values for parent and child
examples.explanation: This demonstrates fork() return values: parent receives child's PID (>0), child receives 0, and failure returns -1. The parent calls wait() to prevent the child from becoming a zombie.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for fork, O(n) for wait

examples.title: Zombie Process Demonstration
examples.description: Shows how zombie processes are created
examples.explanation: This creates a zombie process: child terminates with exit() but parent doesn't call wait() immediately. During the 10-second sleep, the child appears as zombie (state 'Z') in process list. After wait(), it's cleaned up.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is process table size for cleanup

examples.title: Orphan Process Demonstration
examples.description: Shows how orphan processes are created and handled
examples.explanation: The parent terminates before the child completes. The child becomes an orphan and is adopted by init (PID 1). After sleep, the child's getppid() will return 1 (init).
